zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith於1370年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯時，他的父親Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwell45歲，母親Joanna Moray Countess of Douglas39歲。他於1387年與Princess Elizabeth Stewart結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1440年2月在他的家鄉享年70歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭素斯 藍納可爾道格拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith于1370年出生在苏格兰米德洛迪安達爾凱斯时，他的父亲Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwell45岁，母亲Joanna Moray Countess of Douglas39岁。他于1387年与Princess Elizabeth Stewart结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1440年2月在他的家乡享年70岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰素斯 藍納可爾道格拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith was born in 1370, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, his father, Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwell, was 45 and his mother, Joanna Moray Countess of Douglas, was 39. He married Princess Elizabeth Stewart in 1387. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in February 1440, in his hometown, at the age of 70, and was buried in Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith est né en 1370, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwell, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Joanna Moray Countess of Douglas, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Princess Elizabeth Stewart en 1387. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en février 1440, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeithが1370年にスコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで生まれた時，父Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwellは45歳，母Joanna Moray Countess of Douglasは39歳であった。1387年にPrincess Elizabeth Stewartと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1440年2月に故郷で70歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドソウス ラナルクシュアイアドウグラスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith(이)가 1370년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwell(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Joanna Moray Countess of Douglas(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1387년 Princess Elizabeth Stewart(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1440년 2월 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 더글라스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Douglas 1st Lord of Dalkeith родился в 1370 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Archibald Douglas 3rd Earl of Douglas Earl of Wigtown Lord of Galloway and Bothwell было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Joanna Moray Countess of Douglas, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Elizabeth Stewart в 1387 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер февраль 1440 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности доуглас, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
